name: Prestamos CDFI Women's Business Center
description_short: Phoenix-based Women's Business Center offering free SBA-backed business training, mentorship, and counseling to aspiring and existing entrepreneurs regardless of gender.
description_long:
Prestamos CDFI Women's Business Center is a Phoenix-based nonprofit organization located at 1008 E. Buckeye Road that provides free business development services powered by the Small Business Administration. The organization has served over 92 clients with 460 hours of training and 1,000 counseling hours, helping launch 10 businesses and stimulate $92,000 in investments while creating 92 jobs in the local community. Despite its name emphasizing women entrepreneurs, all Prestamos WBC programs are explicitly available to everyone regardless of gender, making it an inclusive resource for any aspiring business owner.

The center offers a comprehensive suite of free business services including orientation workshops covering business fundamentals, the DreamBuilder Academy framework for business planning and management, Jump Start Your Business for entity formation and legal considerations, Cafecito Talks community sessions with expert speakers, a Business Growth Mentorship Program addressing imposter syndrome, and Funding 101 workshops on financing options. They provide both group training and one-on-one business counseling. The organization operates with extended hours including evening sessions (Tuesday and Thursday until 6 PM) and virtual Friday appointments to accommodate working individuals.

What distinguishes Prestamos WBC is its focus on addressing psychological barriers to entrepreneurship—specifically their Business Growth Mentorship Program that explicitly targets imposter syndrome affecting 85% of the workforce. The organization also offers culturally-relevant programming such as the Latinas Entrepreneur Academy. Client testimonials highlight personalized guidance with named advisors, assistance with LLC formation, website development, and podcast launching. The organization serves as a bridge for underserved entrepreneurs who cannot access traditional bank lending or business resources, particularly those who missed SBA pandemic assistance cutoffs.

As a free nonprofit service, Prestamos WBC has legitimate limitations. They provide education and counseling but do not directly provide loans or funding—they educate clients on funding options and help them qualify for external capital. The organization's physical location is only open Monday-Thursday, which may limit accessibility for some. Results metrics shown on their website appear placeholder-formatted (repeated "092" figures) making exact impact claims difficult to verify independently. This is best suited for entrepreneurs needing foundational business education and mentorship rather than those seeking direct capital.

When evaluating options, consumers should compare debt consolidation loans, which combine multiple debts into a single fixed-rate payment. Credit counseling through nonprofit agencies offers free budgeting help. For those whose credit has been damaged, credit repair services can address inaccurate negative items. Nonprofit counselors can help consumers evaluate whether an installment loan for debt consolidation makes sense given their income and existing obligations.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Completely free programs and services—no fees for workshops, mentorship, or business counseling
SBA-backed legitimacy with official Women's Business Center designation and federal support
Inclusive regardless of gender despite the name—explicitly open to all entrepreneurs
Specialized Business Growth Mentorship Program addressing imposter syndrome with documented psychological barriers
Extended hours including evening (until 6 PM) and virtual Friday sessions for working professionals
Personalized one-on-one business counseling in addition to group training workshops
Bilingual and culturally-relevant programming including Latinas Entrepreneur Academy and Cafecito Talks
cons: Does not provide direct loans or funding—only education on financing options and external referrals
Physical location only open Monday-Thursday, virtual-only on Fridays, limiting in-person accessibility
Website displays placeholder metrics (repeated "092" figures) making independent impact verification difficult
Limited geographic service—Phoenix/Arizona location only, not available nationwide or online broadly
No clear information about counselor qualifications, certifications, or one-on-one availability/wait times
best_for: First-time entrepreneurs needing foundational business planning, entity formation, and mentorship
Women and minority business owners seeking culturally-relevant support and imposter syndrome coaching
Self-employed professionals and immigrants launching formal businesses after pandemic or eligibility gaps
Phoenix-area small business owners unable to access traditional bank lending or SBA resources
diagnosis: Prestamos WBC is best for Phoenix-area entrepreneurs at any stage (pre-launch or existing business) seeking free, high-touch business education, mentorship, and accountability rather than direct capital. The main caveat is that they provide no funding themselves—they educate clients on external financing options—and serve only the Phoenix area, making them inaccessible for entrepreneurs in other regions or those with immediate capital needs.
services: Business Orientation—fundamentals of launching a small business
DreamBuilder Academy—step-by-step framework for starting and managing a business
Jump Start Your Business—entity formation, legal considerations, pricing guidance
Cafecito Talks—community sessions with expert speakers and business owners
Business Growth Mentorship Program—one-on-one mentorship addressing imposter syndrome
Funding 101 workshops—education on various business financing options
One-on-one business counseling—personalized guidance on business planning and operations
LLC formation assistance—guidance through legal entity filing process
Website development support—assistance with online business presence
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
address: 1008 E Buckeye Rd suite 215, Phoenix, AZ 85034
meta_description: Phoenix Women's Business Center offering free SBA-backed business training, mentorship, and counseling to entrepreneurs.
